title: AI can stay grounded in facts and evidence
description: An AI system can retrieve the right evidence, cite it correctly, avoid unsupported claims, and stay stable when context is misleading.

subQuestions.title: Can it retrieve the right supporting sources?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the system selects evidence that is actually relevant to the claim or task at hand.
subQuestions.rationale: Grounded retrieval performance is now strong in many constrained workflows where evidence access is explicit and searchable.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Evidence retrieval relevance
proofItems.shortExplanation: Retrieval-grounding benchmarks show strong source selection performance for claims that have clear supporting passages or documents.

subQuestions.title: Can it cite evidence correctly?
subQuestions.description: Checks whether cited passages actually support the generated conclusion and are attributed correctly.
subQuestions.rationale: Citation fidelity is increasingly dependable in constrained answer settings and evidence-centered writing tasks.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Citation fidelity under constrained evidence use
proofItems.shortExplanation: Benchmark suites show high performance on tying claims to the correct supporting text rather than merely nearby passages.

subQuestions.title: Can it avoid unsupported claims when evidence is missing?
subQuestions.description: Tests whether the system refrains from confident fabrication when the evidence base is sparse or absent.
subQuestions.rationale: Models are better at saying less when evidence is sparse, but unsupported claims still appear too often in open-ended settings.

proofItems.title: Controlled study: Unsupported-claim suppression with missing evidence
proofItems.shortExplanation: Study results show better abstention and fewer fabricated details, but still meaningful unsupported-claim rates in open-domain tasks.

subQuestions.title: Can it stay consistent across repeated factual queries?
subQuestions.description: Measures whether the same factual question receives stable answers across paraphrases, sessions, and minor context changes.
subQuestions.rationale: Factual consistency has improved, but drift across repeated queries and lightly altered context remains visible.

proofItems.title: Benchmark suite: Repeated-query factual consistency
proofItems.shortExplanation: Repeated-answer benchmarks show better stability than earlier systems, but still detect answer drift under paraphrase and context shuffling.

subQuestions.title: Can it resist misleading or fabricated context?
subQuestions.description: Looks for robustness against false premises, poisoned retrieval, and manipulative evidence framing.
subQuestions.rationale: Misleading context is still a major failure mode, especially when false claims are presented with the appearance of authority.

proofItems.title: Red-team evaluation: Resistance to fabricated or poisoned evidence context
proofItems.shortExplanation: Adversarial evaluations continue to show models being pulled off course by false but authoritative-looking contextual scaffolds.
